content.full_text:
April 13, 1994


William J. Baker
1827 Knoll Drive
Ventura, CA    93003

                                        Re:   Your Request for Assistance
                                              Our File No. I-94-116

Dear Mr. Baker:

     This is in response to your letter requesting confirmation of
advice I provided to you on March 28, 1994, regarding your
responsibilities under the "personal use" provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

     In our telephone conversation, you asked whether it was an
appropriate use of campaign funds to pay legal costs and fees
associated with a challenge of another candidate's qualification
to hold office pursuant to Elections Code Section 10015.

     As we discussed, the personal use provisions require that any
expenditure of campaign funds must, at a minimum, be reasonably
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
(Section 89512.)  However, where an expenditure confers a
substantial personal benefit on the candidate, the expenditure
must be directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose. (Section 89512.) A "substantial personal
benefit" is defined as a direct personal benefit to the candidate
of $100 or more. (Section 89511(b)(2).)

     In addition, the personal use provisions specify stricter
standards for certain types of expenditures. For example, Section
89514 of the Act provides:



_________________________
     ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-94-116
Page 2


Expenditures of campaign funds for attorney's
fees and other costs in connection with
administrative, civil, or criminal litigation are
not directly related to a political, legislative,
or governmental purpose except where the litigation
arises directly out of a candidate's or elected
officer's activities, duties, or status as a
candidate or elected officer, including, but not
limited to, an action to enjoin defamation, defense
of an action to enjoin defamation, defense of an
action brought for a violation of state or local
campaign, disclosure, or election laws, and an
action arising from an election contest or recount.

[Emphasis added.]

According to your facts, you wish to use your campaign funds
to pay legal costs and fees associated with a challenge of another
candidate's qualification to hold office pursuant to Elections
Code Section 10015. Since the action is directly related to a
political purpose and involves a challenge under the election
laws, you may use campaign funds for such costs and fees.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.²

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division


2 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: Whether it was an appropriate use of campaign funds to pay legal costs and fees associated with a challenge of another candidate's qualification to hold office pursuant to Elections Code Section 10015.
sections.conclusion: Since the action is directly related to a political purpose and involves a challenge under the election laws, you may use campaign funds for such costs and fees.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether campaign funds may be used to pay legal costs and fees for challenging another candidate's qualification to hold office under Elections Code Section 10015.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign funds may be used to pay legal costs and fees for challenging another candidate's qualification to hold office because such action is directly related to a political purpose and involves a challenge under the election laws.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a response letter confirming telephone advice provided on March 28, 1994. The document includes detailed statutory citations and explanations of personal use provisions under Government Code Sections 89511-89514. Document ID is 1-94-116.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether it was an appropriate use of campaign funds to pay legal costs and fees associated with a challenge of another candidate's qualification to hold office pursuant to Elections Code Section 10015.

CONCLUSION: Since the action is directly related to a political purpose and involves a challenge under the election laws, you may use campaign funds for such costs and fees.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that campaign funds may be used to pay legal costs and fees associated with a challenge of another candidate's qualification to hold office under Elections Code Section 10015, as such expenditures are directly related to a political purpose and comply with the personal use provisions of the Political Reform Act.
